How's it going everybody? In this video I'm going to walk you through installing Google Chrome onto your Mac computer. Very important that you install Google Chrome and you use it as you're going through the portal, as you all of the steps and information we teach you, because Google Chrome is what we tested everything. And if you use a different browser, it may not act or look like you expected it to when you're going through tutorials. So it's very important to get Google Chrome and use it. If you already have Google on your Mac, that's fine. Just make sure you open up and use it. Otherwise, watch this video and we'll be through installing it. Below this video, we're going to give you a link to the download of Google Chrome webpage. This is a support page from Google that has all the information. Now they may end up changing the appearance of this page. But you want to look for the section that says install Chrome on Mac. And it should be the list. Once you see that, we're looking for this blue link here that says download the install file. And when you click on that download the installation file link under Chrome, it's going to take you to the download page. They've updated the appearance of this a few, but you should always have this blue download Chrome button right there. Go ahead and hit that Chrome button. It's going to open the terms of service. Make sure you read and agree to those except an install. It's going to go ahead and start the download and depending on your end may take a few moments. That's okay. Now if you're using some look up here at the top right corner, it has this little arrow that says show downloads. Click on that. You're going to see Google Chrome.dmg. That's what the file for. If you're using a different browser at the bottom right, you should have a downloads. And when you click on that, it shows the same in Google Chrome G. It doesn't matter which one you pick. Just look for that file to open up. This verification screen and verification can take a little bit depending on your computer. So it's going to take quite a while. I've already tested this file and I know it will work and skip. But on your side, you do want to let it go through that verification process. Nothing was corrupted when downloaded. Now once a verification start open up this window here. It has a Google Chrome icon at the top and an icons icon below. You're going to click and hold with your mouse on this Google Chrome. And you want to drag it down to applications until you see that green plus sign and then let go. So let me show you that one more time. You click and hold until you see the green plus sign and let go. Once you do that, this is copying Google Chrome to applications. And it may take a few moments. I'm going to go and close out some of those. That way it can hopefully run a bit faster. It doesn't have to waste resources on. And again, mind's an older Mac. Hopefully yours will run a bit faster than this. Now once Chrome is Clickations folder and the applications is where all of the programs are located, then we'll be able to open and then run it. Maybe a couple little extra steps we have to do depending on the settings on your Mac. But I'll go that. So it's finished copying. So I'm going to go ahead on this icon right here. And when I double click, it's going to open up this window for me. And there's my Google Chrome icon. You may need to scroll up or down. But once you find Google Chrome, go ahead and double click on it. That little bouncing Chrome icon in the dock at the bottom. That means it's opening one more time and that's okay. And then you'll see this little pop up. Google Chrome is an application on the internet. Are you sure you want to open it? Now if you see a different pop up saying the app store and you need to change your preferences, that's something handle. So just hold on a moment. I'll show you how to make that setting so that you can open Chrome. So I'm going to go and hit open here. I'm going to open up for you. Now the actual start page manner should be pretty close and it should be pretty similar to what you're used to. You have an ad here that you can either type a website or search in. You have a backup arrow, full refresh. And then many of the Chrome settings are in these little three dots at the top. And you click on that. It's going to open up that menu and you have a lot of different settings there. Now if you tried to open Google Chrome and you saw an error message about how you aren't allowed to that aren't from the app store, you need to open your preferences. Go ahead and open. Put a button on it there or you can click the system preferences. This is in the bottom if you have it inside of your system preferences. Look for security. It's a little house with a safe combination lock on it and double. And then right here we want to be on the general. And if yours has a lock there, go ahead and click on that lock in the password that you use to get into your Mac. And you see now we can change those settings. And so change the start right here is allowed apps downloaded from the app store or only only get Google Chrome. If you get it from the app store and that's not where it's available. What you need to do is change that dot right there. You want to make sure the dot is next to an identified developers. Now again, you should only do this step if it tells you what. So otherwise you're good to go. But if you see that you need an error and you need to allow apps that aren't from the app store to change right there app store and identify developers. Go ahead and close it out. Once you close that out, you should be able to open Google Chrome without an issue. That's it for installing Google Chrome on a Mac. I hope you enjoyed this video and I hope you enjoyed it.
